items: The prior day had produced a modest gain on cooler PPI and solid earnings.
Investors were assessing more corporate results and fresh economic data, but AI/chip leadership was already unstable.
Global semiconductor volatility, especially in South Korea, was an active overnight warning.

intraday_and_postclose_catalysts: Nvidia, Broadcom, Micron and Alphabet fell, overwhelming otherwise positive market breadth.
More S&P 500 stocks rose than fell, but cap-weighted technology weakness controlled the index.
Treasury yields rose and oil's early gain faded.
causal_narrative: This was another concentration-driven decline. The average stock was healthier than the index, but losses in the largest AI and platform companies dominated SPY/SPX. Rising yields amplified valuation pressure, while the failure of oil to sustain gains limited the move from becoming a broader inflation panic.
alternative_explanations: Options and dealer positioning may amplify or oppose the public-news impulse.
Index concentration can make SPY and SPX diverge from equal-weight breadth.
